facts.text: Radburn, a planned community founded 1929 as "a town for the motor age," sits within Fair Lawn and hosts two NJ Transit stations.
Fair Lawn’s historic Dunkerhook Road traces back to a Lenni‑Lenape fishing weir called "slooterdam" built in the Passaic River.
The former Nabisco cookie factory on Fair Lawn Avenue once gave the area the nickname "Cookie‑City" for the scent of freshly baked cookies.
Zerega’s Sons Inc., founded in 1848 in Brooklyn, now produces 100 million pounds of pasta annually and is headquartered in Fair Lawn.
The Peter Garretson House on River Road, built in 1974, is one of Fair Lawn’s eight National Register of Historic Places sites.
Fair Lawn’s Memorial Park neighborhood borders the Passaic River and contains a World War II memorial honoring local veterans.
